title: Connection Reuse, Pools, HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and Concurrency · Go Client Systems · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A platform reliability team. The team needs deadline-aware retry orchestrator with protocol-correct behavior, bounded authority, changed-case tests, and an operational defense. Planned artifact: Deliver Deadline-Aware Retry Orchestrator with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Allocate an end-to-end latency budget across attempts and phases while preserving time for cleanup and fallback. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Limit total attempts across nested callers and prevent retry storms, duplicate effects, and load amplification. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Report which phase and policy ended an operation without exposing secrets or claiming the remote effect is known. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Commit to the mission model · Project Phase Budget Allocation
steps.instruction:
project case — Go stakeholder SDK schedules 24 operations across HTTP/1.1 keep-alive, an HTTP/2 connection limited to 3 streams, and optional HTTP/3. Bound queues and memory, reuse clients, finish bodies, and distinguish transport from semantics. Competency probe: Allocate an end-to-end latency budget across attempts and phases while preserving time for cleanup and fallback. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go Commit to the mission model · Project Phase Budget Allocation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for allocate an end-to-end latency budget across attempts and phases while preserving time for cleanup and fallback. demonstrate the result through go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, roundtripper, and httptest evidence. For Go Commit to the mission model · Project Phase Budget Allocation, keep Phase Budget Allocation connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
